Yet the account then jumps to the rivalry drawing national attention to a faltering NBA, before circling back to the Los Angeles Lakers' three-peat led by Kobe Bryant and Shaquille O'Neal. It next shifts to the 1960s, when the Boston Celtics dominated the NBA by winning eight straight titles and later adding two more consecutive championships, before returning once again to the Lakers' three consecutive championships. The sequence of events is presented in a way that moves back and forth across eras rather than unfolding in a clear order.

Several events are repeated or revisited after later references, creating an inconsistent and jumpy temporal progression.
